title: Proving AI ROI to Your Board: Metrics That Matter
excerpt: Boards want evidence, not anecdotes. These AI engineering metrics help CTOs justify tool spend with numbers leadership trusts.
bodyHtml:
"Developers love Copilot" is not a board slide. After a year of AI tool rollouts, directors expect ROI: cost per shipped feature, adoption curves, and whether spend scales linearly with headcount.

Metrics that resonate with finance

  • Cost per active developer — total AI tool spend ÷ developers using tools weekly
  • Spend vs. budget — trailing 30-day burn with forecast to quarter-end
  • Tool overlap cost — developers on both Cursor and Copilot without incremental output
  • Model mix efficiency — share of spend on premium vs. economy models

Metrics that resonate with engineering

  • PR cycle time before and after rollout (from existing Git metrics)
  • Adoption rate by team and tenure cohort
  • Incidents tied to AI-generated code (track separately—don't conflate)

Package the narrative

Combine spend dashboards with delivery signals your org already trusts. ForgeMeter's AI summaries are designed for exactly this: plain-language paragraphs a CTO can paste into a board deck, with underlying numbers finance can audit.

Get a baseline in one hour

Connect your stack at forgemeter.com, review the first monthly snapshot, and iterate on the metrics your board actually asks about—before the next budget cycle.
